original_abstract: Traffic forecasting as a canonical task of multivariate time series forecasting has been a significant research topic in AI community. To address the spatio-temporal heterogeneity and non-stationarity implied in the traffic stream, in this study, we propose Spatio-Temporal Meta-Graph Learning as a novel Graph Structure Learning mechanism on spatio-temporal data. Specifically, we implement this idea into Meta-Graph Convolutional Recurrent Network (MegaCRN) by plugging the Meta-Graph Learner powered by a Meta-Node Bank into GCRN encoder-decoder. We conduct a comprehensive evaluation on two benchmark datasets (i.e., METR-LA and PEMS-BAY) and a new large-scale traffic speed dataset called EXPY-TKY that covers 1843 expressway road links in Tokyo. Our model outperformed the state-of-the-arts on all three datasets. Besides, through a series of qualitative evaluations, we demonstrate that our model can explicitly disentangle the road links and time slots with different patterns and be robustly adaptive to any anomalous traffic situations. Codes and datasets are available at https://github.com/deepkashiwa20/MegaCRN.
humanized_abstract: In the era of big data and AI, predicting future states of complex and dynamic systems such as smart traffic networks has become an increasingly popular topic in both academic and industry communities. Because of its practical significance, the traffic forecasting, which can be considered as a classical example of multivariate time series forecasting task, has been extensively explored in various AI literature. One major challenge for forecasting the traffic speed lies in the spatio-temporal heterogeneity and non-stationarity of the traffic stream. To tackle the underlying challenging characteristics, in this paper, we introduce a new Graph Structure Learning method named Spatio-Temporal Meta-Graph Learning and demonstrate its effectiveness by applying it to traffic speed forecasting, which is achieved by extending the Meta-Graph Convolutional Recurrent Network (MegaCRN) proposed in our previous work, where we introduce a novel learner called Meta-Graph Learner (Meta-Graph) powered by a Meta-Node Bank. Extensive experiments on the popular benchmark datasets METR-LA and PEMS-BAY, as well as a newly constructed large-scale traffic speed dataset called EXPY-TKY which covers 1843 expressway road links in Tokyo area, confirm the competitiveness of the proposed method against the state-of-the-art methods on all three tested datasets. We also demonstrate the forecasting ability and robustness of our model by showing its capacity to 1) explore the inner relationships of different road links and distinguish the unique temporal patterns of different time slots in a day, 2) adapt to anomalous or unpredictable traffic behaviors at any time. Experiment code and the EXPY-TKY dataset are available at: https://github.com/deepkashiwa20/MegaCRN.

document.output: In the era of big data and AI, predicting future states of complex and dynamic systems such as smart traffic networks has become an increasingly popular topic in both academic and industry communities. Because of its practical significance, the traffic forecasting, which can be considered as a classical example of multivariate time series forecasting task, has been extensively explored in various AI literature. Extensive experiments on the popular benchmark datasets METR-LA and PEMS-BAY, as well as a newly constructed large-scale traffic speed dataset called EXPY-TKY which covers 1843 expressway road links in Tokyo area, confirm the competitiveness of the proposed method against the state-of-the-art methods on all three tested datasets. We also demonstrate the forecasting ability and robustness of our model by showing its capacity to 1) explore the inner relationships of different road links and distinguish the unique temporal patterns of different time slots in a day, 2) adapt to anomalous or unpredictable traffic behaviors at any time. Experiment code and the EXPY-TKY dataset are available at: https://github.com/deepkashiwa20/MegaCRN.
